title: [Feature]: Evaluate an Amazon Redshift sink
problem: Oracle GoldenGate lists Amazon Redshift as a target-only data warehouse. nats-sinks does not yet define a Redshift write strategy, staging model, or idempotency profile.
proposal: Research an Amazon Redshift sink that writes normalized event envelopes through a safe batch path, likely using staging files plus Redshift COPY or parameterized SQL for small volumes. The design should compare direct inserts, COPY from S3, merge/upsert behavior, transaction boundaries, and how to avoid acknowledging messages before warehouse acceptance.
users: Analytics and data warehouse teams moving JetStream events into AWS Redshift for reporting, audit, and downstream analysis.
delivery_semantics: Success may be returned only after Redshift has committed the target rows or after a documented staging-plus-COPY transaction is durable. Duplicate redelivery must be controlled with deterministic keys and merge/upsert logic.
security: Credentials, IAM roles, S3 staging prefixes, table names, and payloads must be redacted. SQL values must use parameters or safe COPY manifests; identifiers require strict allow-list validation.
acceptance: Research selects a safe Redshift ingest pattern.
The design documents direct versus staged writes and commit boundaries.
Idempotent merge or insert-ignore behavior is specified.
Unit tests use fakes and no AWS network calls.
Docs include least-privilege IAM and warehouse cost guidance.
tests: Add unit tests for config validation, SQL/COPY manifest construction, identifier validation, duplicate decisions, and error classification. Gate live Redshift tests.
documentation: Add Redshift sink documentation and update roadmap and sink framework pages.
closeout: Close after release of the research decision or implementation.
